title: Listening - Part 4: Relocating for Retirement
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
topic: five people discussing their decisions to move to a different country for their retirement

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I’d worked in the City of London for forty years, and frankly, I was exhausted. [21]The main reason I chose to retire to the south of France was to finally enjoy a climate that didn't involve constant rain and grey skies[/21]. I wanted to spend my days outside. It’s been wonderful, but I wasn't prepared for the bureaucracy. [26]The sheer complexity of the French healthcare and tax systems has been a major source of stress[/26]. You can't just walk into a clinic; there’s a mountain of paperwork for everything. I’ve had to hire a translator just to deal with my local council, which has been an unexpected and quite significant expense that I hadn't budgeted for. || Speaker 2: My husband and I always knew we wanted to move to Thailand. [22]The incredibly low cost of living there meant we could afford a lifestyle that would have been impossible on our pensions back in the UK[/22]. We have a beautiful villa and a pool now. However, being so far away has its downsides. [27]What I find most difficult is the emotional toll of missing out on my grandchildren's daily lives and milestones[/27]. Video calls are great, but they aren't the same as a hug. I find myself feeling quite guilty whenever they mention a school play or a birthday that I can't attend. It’s a trade-off that I’m still trying to come to terms with. || Speaker 3: I spent my career as a travel writer, so I never really felt 'at home' anywhere. [23]I chose to retire to a small island in Greece because I wanted to be part of a community where life moved at a much slower, more traditional pace[/23]. I wanted to escape the modern world. It’s been everything I hoped for, but the practicalities are tough. [28]The most challenging aspect is the limited access to specialized medical care as I get older[/28]. If anything serious happens, it’s a long boat trip and then a flight to Athens. It makes you feel quite vulnerable, and I’ve had to become much more proactive about my health and fitness to minimize the risks. || Speaker 4: I’ve always been a passionate sailor, so [24]moving to the Caribbean was a logical choice to allow me to pursue my hobby every single day[/24]. The water is my home. I thought I’d be in paradise, but the reality of the climate is harsh. [29]The constant, intense humidity has had a surprisingly negative effect on my energy levels and overall motivation[/29]. Some days, it’s just too hot to even go out on the boat, which defeats the whole purpose of the move! I spend a lot more time indoors under the air conditioning than I ever expected, and I’ve had to adjust my expectations of what an 'active' retirement looks like. || Speaker 5: I’d always felt like a bit of an outsider in my own country. [25]I moved to a vibrant city in Mexico because I was drawn to the warmth and openness of the local culture[/25]. I wanted to feel like I belonged somewhere. The community has been incredibly welcoming, but I’ve faced an unexpected hurdle. [30]I’ve found it surprisingly hard to maintain my old friendships back home, as people eventually stop calling when you’re in a different time zone[/30]. You realize that 'out of sight, out of mind' is a very real thing. I’ve had to work twice as hard to build a new social circle here, which can be exhausting at sixty-five. It’s a fresh start, but it’s definitely not a lazy one.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21–25, choose from the list (A–H) each speaker's primary reason for moving abroad.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 wanted to enjoy a better climate and spend more time outside.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 highlights the low cost of living allowing for a better lifestyle on a pension.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 wanted to be part of a community with a slower, more traditional pace of life.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 moved to pursue his passion for sailing every day.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 was drawn to the warmth and openness of the local culture.

options.A: to benefit from lower day-to-day expenses
options.B: to experience a different social atmosphere
options.C: to be closer to their adult children
options.D: to escape a high-stress professional environment
options.E: to live in more favorable weather conditions
options.F: to take advantage of better healthcare
options.G: to adopt a more relaxed way of life
options.H: to engage more fully in a personal interest

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26–30, choose from the list (A–H) what each speaker mentions as a significant difficulty they encountered.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 mentions the complexity of the healthcare and tax systems and the mountain of paperwork.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 finds the emotional toll of missing her grandchildren's milestones most difficult.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 mentions the limited access to specialized medical care on the island.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 notes that the intense humidity has negatively affected his energy and motivation.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 mentions how hard it is to maintain old friendships because of the distance and time zones.

options.B: the absence of close family members
options.D: a lack of suitable local infrastructure
options.E: the high cost of property maintenance
options.F: the physical impact of the local environment
options.G: the gradual erosion of long-term social ties
options.H: difficulty in mastering the local language
